The Story

The police closed the case.
Jenny refused to.

Two months of silence. A forest that should not exist. And a world hidden inside the space between the tick and the tock of a watch — waiting for someone brave enough to step inside.

"NOE!!!" Her cry tore through the room, raw and desperate. She sat upright in bed, chest heaving. Everything looked normal. But Noe was gone.

— Chapter 2 · The Closing Case
The Story

A world hidden between
the tick and the tock

For his sister Jenny, silence is not an answer. Alongside Dev — a quiet protector with a past he never speaks of — she follows black feathers, a whispering lullaby, and symbols that should not exist.

Time is not a line. It is a fabric. And it has been torn.

In the fractures between seconds lives a hidden world. An ancient power is waking. She was burned to ash. She swore she would return.

Get the Book

"The air split apart… a shimmering portal opened. Inside, time itself flowed backward — stars falling, the earth spinning, everything pulled into an unseen force."

— Chapter 1 · The Lost Path
Read the Opening
Chapter 1 — The Lost Path · Velmora City

The hour of twilight had come. The sky still glowed faintly, but the streets had already surrendered to shadow.

In a narrow, deserted alley, a thin boy had been surrounded by a few others. Their hands were empty, but their intent was sharper than any blade.

"Why aren't you running now? You wanted to act like a hero, right?"

A fist slammed into his stomach — the air burst from his lungs… And then — he burst beyond the city's last wall and stumbled onto the lonely path leading toward the forest.

The shouts still echoed — until they suddenly stopped.

One muttered: "Not in there… not at night."

Something heavier pressed on his chest —
the silence itself.

What happens next changes everything. Read the full book →
The Villain

Her name isOMBRE

She has been stealing time from the living for centuries.
She was burned to ash.
She swore she would return.
She already has.
And she already knows Jenny's name.
"So… the Chosen have finally reached the door."

"Family in chains is not family. And power without love… is poison."

— Dev, Chapter 15 · The Final Battle
☽   ☾

"Come now, it is time to sleep…
The night is calling you…
I am standing right beside you,
The doorway of dreams is open wide…"

— heard in the forest, in daylight, Chapter III
From the Pages

"To be Chosen is not honour — it is burden. The choice was never yours. But the decisions will be."

— Simus · Chapter 5

"After Mum and Dad were gone, he was everything to me. If I lose him too… then I'll be completely alone."

— Jenny · Chapter 2

"Family in chains is not family. And power without love… is poison."

— Dev · Chapter 15

"I'm sorry, Jenny…" The stone pulsed once — faint but alive. And the room sank back into silence.

— Dev · Chapter 17 · The ending you won't see coming
The Artefact

The Sigil of Silence

Buried in the forest roots. Three crescent shapes carved back-to-back. When Dev's fingers closed around it, something inside vibrated — subtle but undeniable.

It was not just metal. It knew them. It had been waiting for them.

It was the key to everything.

"You were both born in the crack of time… That is why you were chosen. The Chosen."

— Simus · Chapter 5 · Cracks in Time
Meet the Chosen

Four people. One fractured world.
Each hiding something.

Jenny Noe's sister. Will not stop. Ever. "You people can give up. But I won't."
Dev Quiet protector. Carries a secret. "I don't let anyone rush ahead of me. Not again."
Vorrak The fighter. The last line of defence. "She is NOT alone!!"
Victoria Half light. Half shadow. All secrets. "My mother would never ask me to abandon them."
The Four Chosen — Together
Victoria · Jenny · Vorrak · Dev
The World

Velmora City

Where the city ends. And the forest begins.

A complete dark fantasy world

The Space Between
A world hidden inside the fractures of time itself, where reality no longer applies.

About the Author

Rahul Dev

Architect · Author · Creator

By profession, an architect and builder who has spent years designing structures, managing large-scale housing and commercial projects across India. By passion — a storyteller who builds worlds that exist between the seconds of a clock.

Fear of the Lost World is his debut novel — the first of a four-book dark fantasy saga. Rahul writes with the belief that stories are more than words. They are living worlds where readers can lose themselves, and perhaps, find themselves too.

"This is not just my story anymore. It is yours too."
